note: All figures sourced from official government documents. See sourceUrl per entity.

entities.name: Durham County

FY2025-26.note: Source: Durham County FY2025-26 Adopted Budget Summary

FY2024-25.note: Source: Durham County FY2024-25 Adopted Budget Summary

fundingSources.name: Property Tax
fundingSources.note: Ad valorem tax at 55.42¢ per $100 assessed value: Durham County's primary revenue source

fundingSources.name: Sales & Use Tax
fundingSources.note: County share of NC sales taxes (Articles 39, 40, 42, 43, and 46) distributed by the state

fundingSources.name: State & Federal Revenue
fundingSources.note: State-shared revenues, Medicaid reimbursements, federal program grants, and 911 fees

fundingSources.name: Charges for Services
fundingSources.note: Register of Deeds fees, health department charges, parks and recreation fees, permits

fundingSources.name: Other Revenue
fundingSources.note: Investment earnings, appropriated fund balance, miscellaneous receipts

entities.fundingSourcesNote: General Fund revenue breakdown (~$690M). Percentages are approximate based on the adopted budget summary. Property tax calculated from 55.42¢/$100 rate and county assessed value. Source: dconc.gov/Budget-and-Management-Services

areas.name: Education
areas.description: County funding passed through to Durham Public Schools and Durham Technical Community College. Includes current expense, capital outlay, debt service, and ARPA support.
areas.sourceNote: Source: Durham County FY2025-26 Budget Summary; Education.pdf

departments.name: DPS – Current Expense
departments.description: Funds daily school operations: teacher and staff salaries, instructional materials, utilities, and support services for Durham Public Schools.

departments.name: DPS – Capital Outlay
departments.description: Pays for school building repairs, equipment replacements, and smaller facility improvements not covered by bond projects.

departments.name: DPS – ARPA Title I
departments.description: Federal American Rescue Plan funds targeted to low-income students in Title I schools.

departments.name: DTCC – Current Expense
departments.description: Operating support for Durham Technical Community College, the county's two-year public college serving approximately 14,000 students.

departments.name: DTCC – Capital Outlay
departments.description: Funding for DTCC facilities maintenance, equipment, and capital improvements.

areas.name: Administration
areas.description: County management, finance, human resources, information technology, legal, and general government operations.
areas.sourceNote: Source: Durham County FY2025-26 Budget Summary page

departments.name: County Manager's Office
departments.description: Executive leadership and policy coordination across all county departments.

departments.name: Finance & Budget
departments.description: Financial management, accounting, purchasing, and budget administration for the entire county.

departments.name: Human Resources
departments.description: Employee recruitment, benefits administration, workforce development, and labor relations.

departments.name: Information Technology
departments.description: County-wide technology infrastructure, cybersecurity, digital services, and help desk support.

departments.name: County Attorney
departments.description: Legal counsel, contract review, and representation for the Board of Commissioners and county departments.

departments.name: Register of Deeds
departments.description: Recording and preservation of land records, birth/death certificates, and other official documents.

areas.name: Human Services
areas.description: Social services, public health, mental health, child welfare, aging services, and community assistance programs.
areas.sourceNote: Source: Durham County FY2025-26 Budget Summary page

departments.name: Durham County Social Services
departments.description: Child protective services, foster care, adoption, adult services, and economic assistance programs including Medicaid, Food & Nutrition, and Work First.

departments.name: Public Health
departments.description: Disease surveillance, immunizations, maternal and child health, environmental health inspections, and community health programs.

departments.name: Mental Health / LME-MCO
departments.description: County support for behavioral health services coordinated through the Local Management Entity: Managed Care Organization.

departments.name: Aging & In-Home Care
departments.description: Senior centers, Meals on Wheels, transportation assistance, and in-home aide services for older adults.

departments.name: Community & Family Services
departments.description: Housing assistance, utility aid, emergency relief, and partnerships with nonprofits serving Durham families.

areas.name: Public Safety
areas.description: Sheriff's Office, Emergency Medical Services, Emergency Management, and Animal Control.
areas.sourceNote: Source: Durham County FY2025-26 Budget Summary page

departments.name: Durham County Sheriff's Office
departments.description: Patrol, criminal investigations, courthouse security, civil process, and operation of the Durham County Jail.

departments.name: Emergency Medical Services
departments.description: Countywide 911 ambulance response, paramedic staffing, and medical first response from 10 EMS stations.

departments.name: Emergency Management
departments.description: Disaster preparedness, hazard planning, and coordination of county-wide emergency response.

departments.name: Animal Control Services
departments.description: Animal shelter operations, adoption programs, humane enforcement, and public safety response to animal complaints.

areas.name: Debt Service
areas.description: Principal and interest payments on bonds and loans, including DPS school construction debt, DTCC debt, and county facilities debt.
areas.sourceNote: Source: Durham County FY2025-26 Approved Budget Summary PDF

departments.name: DPS-related Debt
departments.description: Bond payments for Durham Public Schools construction and renovation projects: including school building bonds approved by voters.

departments.name: DTCC-related Debt
departments.description: Bond repayment for Durham Technical Community College facility construction and improvements.

departments.name: County Facilities
departments.description: Debt payments on county-owned buildings, the Judicial Building, detention center, and other infrastructure.

departments.name: NCMLS-related Debt
departments.description: Payments for the NC Medical Examiners office and related county-shared facilities.

areas.name: Capital Projects
areas.description: Capital Finance Plan Fund: major facility construction, renovation, and infrastructure improvements funded by bonds and pay-as-you-go capital.
areas.sourceNote: Source: Durham County FY2025-26 Approved Budget Summary PDF

departments.name: School Construction & Renovation
departments.description: New school buildings, major renovations, and HVAC/roofing replacements at Durham Public Schools facilities.

departments.name: County Facility Projects
departments.description: Improvements to county-owned buildings including the Detention Center, Human Services complex, and administrative offices.

departments.name: Parks & Greenways
departments.description: Trail construction, park improvements, greenway extensions, and recreation facility upgrades funded by the parks bond.

departments.name: Technology Infrastructure
departments.description: Major IT investments, fiber connectivity, and digital infrastructure capital projects.

areas.name: Culture & Recreation
areas.description: Durham County Public Library system, parks, and recreation facilities.
areas.sourceNote: Source: Durham County FY2025-26 Budget Summary page

departments.name: Durham County Public Library
departments.description: Main library and branch locations across Durham County: book lending, digital resources, programming, and community meeting space.

departments.name: Parks & Recreation
departments.description: County-operated parks, athletic fields, greenways, nature preserves, and recreation programming.

areas.name: Economic Development
areas.description: Economic and physical development, planning, and community development initiatives.
areas.sourceNote: Source: Durham County FY2025-26 Budget Summary page

departments.name: Planning Department
departments.description: Land use planning, zoning administration, subdivision review, and the county's comprehensive plan.

departments.name: Economic Development Office
departments.description: Business recruitment, retention, workforce partnerships, and support for Durham's economic growth strategy.

departments.name: Community Development
departments.description: Affordable housing programs, HOME funds, CDBG grants, and neighborhood revitalization initiatives.

areas.name: Environmental Protection
areas.description: Soil & Water Conservation, environmental health, and related natural resource programs.
areas.sourceNote: Source: Durham County FY2025-26 Budget Summary page

departments.name: Soil & Water Conservation
departments.description: Watershed protection, erosion control assistance for property owners, and agricultural conservation programs.

departments.name: Environmental Health
departments.description: Restaurant inspections, well and septic permits, swimming pool inspections, and childcare facility oversight.

departments.name: Cooperative Extension
departments.description: NC State/NC A&T cooperative extension providing agricultural, horticultural, and 4-H youth programming.

areas.name: Transportation
areas.description: County contribution to public transit and transportation services.
areas.sourceNote: Source: Durham County FY2025-26 Budget Summary page

departments.name: GoDurham / GoTriangle Transit
departments.description: County funding support for GoDurham bus service and GoTriangle regional transit, connecting neighbors to jobs, healthcare, and services.

entities.name: City of Durham

fundingSources.name: Property Tax
fundingSources.note: Ad valorem tax at 43.71¢ per $100 assessed value: the city's largest general fund revenue source

fundingSources.name: Charges & Fees
fundingSources.note: Parks and recreation fees, planning permits, development review charges, and other service fees

fundingSources.name: Sales & Use Tax
fundingSources.note: City share of NC sales taxes (Article 46 and local option sales tax allocations)

fundingSources.name: State & Federal Grants
fundingSources.note: Powell Bill road maintenance funds, federal public safety grants, community development block grants

fundingSources.name: Other Revenue
fundingSources.note: Investment income, fines and forfeitures, utility franchise fees, and miscellaneous receipts

entities.fundingSourcesNote: General Fund revenue breakdown (~$234M). Enterprise funds (water/sewer, stormwater) are separately funded by utility rates charged to customers: not by taxes. Capital Improvement Plan funded through bonds, grants, and reserves. Percentages approximate from FY2025-26 adopted budget. Source: durhamnc.gov/456/Finance

areas.name: Capital Improvement Plan
areas.description: Five-year capital plan for major infrastructure, facilities, and equipment investments. Funded through bonds, grants, and capital reserves: not property taxes.
areas.sourceNote: Source: City of Durham FY2025-26 Budget Approval press release

departments.name: Water & Sewer System
departments.description: Upgrades to water treatment plants, sewer lines, pump stations, and infrastructure to support growth and replace aging pipes.

departments.name: GoDurham Bus Expansion
departments.description: New buses, transit facility improvements, and expanded service routes for the GoDurham city transit system.

departments.name: Stormwater
departments.description: Stormwater drainage improvements, stream restoration, and flood prevention infrastructure to protect neighborhoods.

departments.name: Street Repaving
departments.description: Resurfacing and reconstruction of city streets to maintain the road network and reduce maintenance costs.

departments.name: Parks – Lead Remediation
departments.description: Removal of lead-contaminated soil at city parks to protect children's health: priority project identified in 2024.

departments.name: Sidewalk Repairs
departments.description: Repairs and new sidewalk construction to improve pedestrian safety and ADA accessibility throughout Durham.

departments.name: Other Capital Projects
departments.description: Facilities improvements, technology infrastructure, fire stations, and other capital investments across city departments.

areas.name: City Operations (General Fund)
areas.description: Day-to-day city government operations funded by property taxes, sales taxes, and fees. Covers police, fire, public works, parks, planning, transit support, and general government.
areas.sourceNote: Department figures are approximate estimates from the adopted budget: verify exact amounts at durhamnc.gov/456/Finance.

departments.name: Durham Police Department
departments.description: Law enforcement, 911 dispatch, criminal investigations, community policing, and crime prevention programs.

departments.name: Durham Fire Department
departments.description: Fire suppression, emergency medical first response, hazmat operations, and fire prevention inspections.

departments.name: General Government
departments.description: City Council, City Manager's office, Finance, HR, IT, City Attorney, and other administrative functions.

departments.name: Public Works
departments.description: Street maintenance, trash collection, recycling, stormwater operations, and right-of-way management.

departments.name: Parks & Recreation
departments.description: City parks, recreation centers, pools, athletic programs, and greenway maintenance.

departments.name: City/County Planning
departments.description: Land use planning, zoning, development review, building permits, and inspections for the city and county.

FY2025-26.note: Per-pupil figure = total budget ÷ ~32,000 students. Source: DPS Recommended Budget FY2025-26

areas.name: State Funding
areas.description: North Carolina state appropriations for public school operations, including teacher salary allotments and categorical program funding.
areas.sourceNote: Source: DPS FY2025-26 Superintendent's Recommended Budget: NC State allotments cover ~34.5% of DPS total funding

areas.name: Local (County) Funding
areas.description: Durham County direct appropriation for school operations: current expense, capital outlay, and ARPA Title I. The county also pays $61.8M in DPS-related debt service separately.
areas.sourceNote: Source: Durham County FY2025-26 Education.pdf: Current Expense $219,528,912 + Capital Outlay $5,000,000 + ARPA Title I $723,376

areas.name: Capital Outlay
areas.description: Funding for school construction, renovations, and major equipment, primarily from county bonds.
areas.sourceNote: Source: DPS FY2025-26 Budget: primarily funded through Durham County bond proceeds (2022 bond referendum)

areas.name: Federal Funding
areas.description: Federal grants including Title I (low-income students), Title II (teacher quality), IDEA (special education), and other federal programs.
areas.sourceNote: Source: DPS FY2025-26 Budget: federal funds represent ~6.1% of total DPS funding

areas.name: Child Nutrition
areas.description: Federal and state funding for school meal programs, including free and reduced-price lunch for eligible students.
areas.sourceNote: Source: DPS FY2025-26 Budget: Child Nutrition fund covers school meal programs for ~32,000 students

areas.name: Grants & Other
areas.description: Competitive grants, special revenue, and other non-recurring funding sources.
areas.sourceNote: Source: DPS FY2025-26 Budget: includes ARPA remaining allocations and one-time grant funds

expenditures.name: Instructional Services
expenditures.description: Classroom teachers, instructional assistants, textbooks, curriculum materials, and all direct student learning activities across DPS schools.

departments.name: Classroom Instruction
departments.description: Teacher salaries, benefits, and classroom supplies: the core of daily school operations.

departments.name: Instructional Support Staff
departments.description: Instructional assistants, reading coaches, curriculum specialists, and library/media staff.

departments.name: Student Support Services
departments.description: Counselors, psychologists, social workers, and special education coordinators supporting student needs.

expenditures.name: Capital Outlay
expenditures.description: School construction, renovation, HVAC replacement, roofing, and major equipment purchases funded through county bonds.

expenditures.name: Systemwide Support Services
expenditures.description: Central administration, human resources, finance, school transportation, technology infrastructure, and school-based administrative support.

departments.name: Central Administration
departments.description: Superintendent's office, school board operations, district leadership, and strategic planning.

departments.name: Transportation
departments.description: School bus operations, routing, and driver staffing for transporting students to and from school.

departments.name: Facilities & Operations
departments.description: Building maintenance, custodial services, utilities, and grounds keeping across all DPS campuses.

departments.name: Technology
departments.description: Student and staff devices, network infrastructure, software licenses, and IT support.

expenditures.name: Non-Programmed Services
expenditures.description: Central administration, plant operations, community programs, and contingency reserves.

expenditures.name: Ancillary Services
expenditures.description: Student transportation, extended-day programs, athletics, and other supplemental services.
